summary: A UK customer testimonial about using Cavallo Simple Hoof Boots to keep a 21-year-old thoroughbred comfortable and active while it had abscesses in both hind hooves from wet weather.
A short, light weekend blog post inviting readers to interpret an image and wishing them time in the saddle.
A brief customer testimonial from Kelsee B. about her horse Spirit running fast and keeping Cavallo Sport Hoof Boots on in the snow.
Carole Herder describes quality control at the Cavallo hoof boot factory in China, emphasizing hand inspection, worker craftsmanship, and the CANEI continuous-improvement philosophy.
Carole Herder recounts loading a 40-foot shipping container of Cavallo hoof boots bound for Europe and the USA, and highlights the family-run nature of the China factory team.
Carole Herder profiles Cavallo's outsole supplier Eric Chiu, whose company also supplies Doc Martens, Wolverine, Caterpillar and Ariat, framing him as a quality partner for hoof boot soles.
Joe Camp argues domestic horses are genetically identical to wild horses and need wide-open movement, constant forage, herd life and bare hooves rather than stalls and metal shoes.
A customer testimonial from Jan Kerr praising the Cavallo bridge saddle pad for noticeably improving an older horse's comfort on the first ride.
A short post noting that the horse ridden by Texas Tech's Masked Rider mascot wears Cavallo Simple Hoof Boots when leading the team onto the field.
Carole Herder reflects on horses' vulnerability to predators like cougars and bears in coastal Canada, and on prey-vs-predator dynamics between horses and humans. Includes a video.
Carole Herder draws a comparison between the commitment required for a ski trip and the commitment of transitioning horses out of metal shoes with proper terrain and trimming.
A narrative by Vaughn Wilson (submitted by Joe Camp) recounting a visit to Joe and Kathleen Camp's Tennessee farm and their adopted BLM mustang mare Saffron and her foal Stormy.
